bibliography:
Sergio Guerreiro (2015). Introducão a Engenharia de Software, In Portuguese, ISBN: 9789727227952, FCA editora de Informatica Lda, Lisbon, Portugal
	
	
	Carvalho, Nuno Ponces & Bernardo, Maria do Rosário (2020). Gestão de Projetos - As práticas no contexto organizacional. 1ª edição, Edições Sílabo, Lisboa.
	
	
	Sommerville, I., Software Engineering, 10th edition, Addison Wesley, 2016
methodology: The UC uses the expository method, combined with the inclusion of practical cases, and also contact with entities in the software industry through face-to-face presentations on best practices in the development of modern software projects.
objectives:
Using the process of developing software, based on their life cycle.

Use the methods, techniques and tools, within a context of Software Engineering.

Using models that allow you to specify, design, implement and maintain software systems

Evaluate and ensure the quality of a software system

Motivating for developing programs with engineering, which integrates the technological aspects of computing with the social and human costs of construction products
organicUnit: ECATI - School of Communication, Architecture, Arts and Information Technologies
programme:
·     Introduction to Software Engineering Complements: Problems and Applications

·     Typology of IT projects

·     Planning the scope and schedule of a SW engineering project

·     Organization of a SW engineering project. Roles, responsibilities and communication plan and risk analysis.

·     Agile software development process models

·     Business case - SW engineering project

·     Identification of strategies for software maintenance

·     Portfolio and demand management for software projects

·     Improvement of the software development process

·     Trends in Software Engineering
presentation: UC intends to complement students' knowledge in the software engineering component with a special focus on software engineering process and agile project management.

Época Normal (Avaliação contínua) = Assiduidade (10%) + 2 Testes (20%+20%) + Projeto/Caso de Uso (50%)

Época Recurso = Caso de Uso (50%) + Exame Teórico (50%)

Época Especial = Caso de Uso (50%) + Exame Teórico (50%)

Notas: 

  • A nota média dos dois testes deverá ser superior a 8.
  • A nota do Projeto/Caso de Uso deverá ser superior a 9.5.
